title: [Bug]: RetryPolicy direct construction accepts unknown mode strings
summary: The RetryPolicy dataclass relies on type hints for mode strings but does not enforce them at runtime.
observed: A direct RetryPolicy instance with an unknown backoff mode is accepted and falls into exponential behavior.
expected: RetryPolicy must enforce allowed backoff and jitter modes during construction.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs RetryPolicy with an unknown backoff mode.
impact: Embedding applications can silently get a different retry algorithm than the one they intended.
delivery_semantics: The fix keeps retry decisions explicit without changing commit-then-ack ordering.
security: Failing closed on policy enums makes failure behavior easier to audit.
acceptance: Valid configured modes continue to work.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, retry tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update retry documentation or release notes if runtime policy validation is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
